channel_title: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
title: Riverside Take 04 Feb 3 2025 from Nate
executive_summary: OpenAI has launched Deep Research, a tool that generates detailed reports in 30 minutes with citations, initially available on the Pro plan but soon to be free. In a significant announcement, Sam Altman and SoftBank's Masayoshi Son revealed that artificial general intelligence (AGI) will be formally announced in Japan within two years, while OpenAI develops a Japan-exclusive AI model named Crystal for local companies.
bullets: OpenAI launched Deep Research, a tool that takes up to 30 minutes to search the web and generate detailed reports with citations. This tool is crucial for researchers and professionals who need reliable information quickly, much like a librarian who can find the exact book you need in a matter of minutes.

Sam Altman and Masayoshi Son announced that AGI will be unveiled in Japan within the next two years. This is a bold statement that indicates a significant milestone in AI development, similar to how a scientist announces a groundbreaking discovery that could change the world.

OpenAI is developing an AI model named Crystal, specifically for Japanese companies. This model will operate within a company's firewall, optimizing code and even listening to phone calls, like a personal assistant who knows your preferences and can help streamline your daily tasks.

SoftBank is making a major investment in OpenAI beyond the Stargate project, aiming to secure Japan's leadership role in AGI. This investment is akin to a country investing in its military to ensure it remains a global superpower, emphasizing the importance of AI in future economies.

Deep Research's full GPT-4.0 model improved OpenAI's score on Humanity's Last Exam from 13% to 25% accuracy in just two days. This rapid improvement highlights the tool's effectiveness, much like a student who suddenly understands a subject after a few focused study sessions.

Masayoshi Son made a peculiar comment stating that AI won't eat people because it doesn't need protein. This odd remark caught even Sam Altman off guard, illustrating how discussions about AI can sometimes take unexpected turns, like a serious meeting suddenly veering into absurdity.

Sam Altman claimed that Deep Research already performs a 'single-digit percentage' of economically valuable white-collar work. This indicates that AI is beginning to take on tasks traditionally done by humans, similar to how calculators replaced manual calculations in offices.

The announcement of AGI in Japan is a strategic move that could position the country as a leader in AI technology. This is comparable to how Silicon Valley became the tech hub of the world, attracting talent and investment.

The Crystal AI model will not only optimize code but also listen to calls, which raises questions about privacy and security. This is like having a smart home device that can hear everything you say, making it crucial to consider where to draw the line on surveillance.

OpenAI's partnership with SoftBank is focused on securing Japan's role in the global AI landscape. This partnership is similar to how countries form alliances to strengthen their positions in international politics.

Deep Research is currently available on the Pro plan but will soon be offered for free, making it accessible to a wider audience. This democratization of technology is like giving everyone access to a library that was once exclusive to a few.

The development of AI tools like Deep Research indicates a shift in how research and information gathering will be conducted in the future. This is akin to how the internet transformed communication, making information available at our fingertips.
key_takeaways: Start using Deep Research as soon as it becomes available for free to enhance your research capabilities. This tool can significantly speed up your information-gathering process, and missing out means you might fall behind in the fast-paced AI landscape.

Prepare for the AGI announcement in Japan within the next two years by staying updated on AI developments. Understanding the implications of AGI will be crucial for your future career, and ignoring this could leave you unprepared for industry changes.

Consider the ethical implications of AI models like Crystal that listen to calls. Establish clear guidelines on privacy and data security in your projects, as neglecting these concerns could lead to significant legal and reputational risks.

technical_terms.Deep Research: A powerful research tool launched by OpenAI that generates detailed reports in 30 minutes. Think of it like a supercharged librarian who can find and summarize information faster than you can blink.
technical_terms.AGI: Artificial General Intelligence, a type of AI that can understand, learn, and apply knowledge across a wide range of tasks, similar to how a human can adapt to different situations and challenges.
technical_terms.Crystal: An AI model being developed by OpenAI specifically for Japanese companies, designed to optimize code and listen to calls. Like a personal assistant that knows your work inside out and helps you improve it.
technical_terms.Stargate project: A major initiative by SoftBank related to AI development, representing a significant investment in technology. Think of it like a launching pad for a rocket, designed to propel advancements in AI.
technical_terms.GPT-4.0: The fourth version of OpenAI's Generative Pre-trained Transformer, a language model that can understand and generate human-like text. Like a highly skilled writer who can produce essays on any topic with ease.
technical_terms.Humanity's Last Exam: A test designed to evaluate AI's understanding and reasoning capabilities, used to measure progress in AI development. Imagine it as a final exam for students, determining their readiness for the real world.
technical_terms.Firewall: A security system that protects a network by controlling incoming and outgoing traffic. Think of it like a security guard at a building entrance, only allowing authorized people to enter.
technical_terms.White-collar work: Jobs that typically involve office work and require mental rather than physical labor, such as research or administration. Like the difference between a factory worker and a corporate manager, both vital but in different ways.
technical_terms.Investment: The act of putting money into something with the expectation of gaining a profit or benefit in the future. It's like planting a seed in the ground, hoping it will grow into a fruitful tree.
technical_terms.Economic impact: The effect that an event or policy has on the economy, influencing factors like employment and productivity. Think of it like a stone thrown into a pond, creating ripples that affect everything around it.
